subject: Public Health
title: Exercise as Mental Health Medicine
description: A quick, practical guide to Exercise as Mental Health Medicine.

hook.text: A 30-minute walk can reduce anxiety as effectively as a dose of medication—with no side effects.

buildup.text: Exercise releases endorphins, reduces cortisol, and promotes neuroplasticity—the brain's ability to rewire.

discovery.text: Regular physical activity reduces depression risk by 25-30% across all age groups and cultures.

twist.text: Depression itself saps motivation to exercise—creating a vicious cycle that's hard to break alone.

climax.text: Prescribing exercise alongside therapy and medication produces better outcomes than any single treatment.

punchline.text: Movement is medicine the body prescribes to the mind.

quiz.question: By how much does regular exercise reduce depression risk?
